analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: BS implied vol from voucher mid vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; vega at implied sigma; vega-weighted mean IV time series vs extract 1-tick return. Spread (ask1−bid1) by voucher. TTE: DTE_open=8−csv_day; DTE_eff=DTE_open−(timestamp//100)/10000; T=DTE_eff/365 per round3description.txt + combined_analysis winding (same as plot_iv_smile_round3). Backtest merged timestamps: csv_day = timestamp//1e6, local_ts = timestamp%1e6.
used_to_decide: Negative correlation (~−0.38) between change in vega-weighted IV and concurrent extract return suggests tilting quotes with extract momentum is directionally consistent with surface co-movement. Mean spread rises for low strikes (VEV_4000 ~21 vs VEV_6500 ~1), so prefer tight inside quotes on liquid strikes and avoid naive wide synthetic spreads. v0’s directional extract leg was abandoned after backtest showed it dominated losses; v2+ use integer shift from inside quotes so OTM strikes still quote.

analysis_description: Greek/microstructure ranking across Round 3 tapes: per-strike mean vega from smile model and mean spread; computed vega_per_spread and return correlation with extract. Top efficiency strikes: 5400, 5300, 5500, 5200. Tested strike-filtered variants (v5/v6/v8) and found severe fill degradation under worse-trade matching; all-strike quoting (v4/v9) remained better.
used_to_decide: Despite high vega-per-spread on mid strikes, restricting traded strikes reduced participation and PnL (v5/v6=0). This falsifies the 'aggressive strike filter' extension for current quote style under this backtester. The concept remains valid as all-strike surface tilt (v4/v9) where extract-driven shift still improves over naive variants.
